hypothesis: Reproduce the best phase-6 direct keepalive discovery result before phase-7 claims.
notes: Anchor matched the phase-6 discovery result exactly on test: overall=0.5472, route=0.5960, fq_acc=0.3349, fq_route=0.4472, fq_exit=93.57. Base verification stayed close (0.5540/0.5905/0.3382/0.4358/92.86), but locked confirmations shifted behavior: full-locked improved overall/fq_acc yet reduced fq_route (0.6973/0.5853/0.4799/0.3255/126.99) and finalquery-heavy showed similar fq_acc with fq_route 0.3354. Discovery remains unstable even when the seed reproduces.

hypothesis: Reproduce the best verified weak-basin staged-recovery anchor before phase-7 pipeline work.
notes: Anchor matched the phase-6 weak-basin sinkcore recovery numerically on test: overall=0.9274, route=0.8981, fq_acc=0.8777, fq_route=0.8369, fq_exit=116.85. Base verification stayed close (0.9173/0.8870/0.8643/0.8155/115.62), and both locked confirmations stayed strong: full-locked 0.9323/0.9033/0.8991/0.8583/118.06, finalquery-heavy 0.9053/0.8659/0.8859/0.8395/116.46.

hypothesis: Reproduce the strongest keepalive-basin memory-only reopen result before phase-7 recovery mapping.
notes: Anchor matched the phase-6 memory-only reopen result exactly on test: overall=0.9613, route=0.9323, fq_acc=0.9826, fq_route=0.9739, fq_exit=126.13. Verification stayed very close on base (0.9629/0.9300/0.9813/0.9699/126.01) and remained strong on both locked confirmations: full-locked 0.9648/0.9329/0.9813/0.9699/126.03, finalquery-heavy 0.9733/0.9580/0.9811/0.9745/126.03.

hypothesis: Final-query-only keepalive supervision might widen the direct discovery basin by focusing the sticky controller on the hard mode.
notes: Completed scout was a stronger negative than the stale ledger note had claimed. Test overall=0.2549, route=0.0632, fq_acc=0.2520, fq_route=0.0000, fq_exit=15.92. The final-query-wait-only shaping produced high delay mass on average but still failed completely on durable final-query routing.

hypothesis: A direct monotone wait controller might discover later exits more easily than the current sticky keepalive path.
notes: Completed scout stayed in the early-exit regime. Test overall=0.4236, route=0.1885, fq_acc=0.2450, fq_route=0.0000, fq_exit=5.06. The monotone/direct gate improved neither final-query timing nor route match.

hypothesis: Longer weak-basin memory+readout reopening may turn the already-rescuable route structure into a stronger recovery pipeline than the phase-6 baseline.
notes: Completed scout produced a materially stronger weak-basin recovery than the old forceoracle and exitselect boundaries: overall=0.7689, route=0.6852, fq_acc=0.5943, fq_route=0.4405, fq_exit=91.47, best_val=0.7617. Still below the phase-6 weak sinkcore anchor, but strong enough to justify an exact rerun. Note: results dir keeps the old seed973 naming convention, but the actual config seed is 1102.

hypothesis: Adding explicit final-query release supervision may keep the sticky controller in the late-wait regime without reverting to premature exit.
notes: Completed scout stayed outside the keepalive basin: overall=0.4919, route=0.3255, fq_acc=0.2426, fq_route=0.0000, fq_exit=18.13, best_val=0.4932. Release supervision delayed exits a bit relative to earlier direct attempts, but still failed to create real final-query route structure.

hypothesis: Stronger persistence scales and a longer schedule may turn the sticky keepalive family from a lucky seed into a wider discovery basin.
notes: Completed scout remained outside the keepalive basin despite stronger persistence and longer schedule: overall=0.5693, route=0.4066, fq_acc=0.2667, fq_route=0.0000, fq_exit=23.81, best_val=0.5679. This is a cleaner negative than releaseaux because it had more training budget and stronger control scales.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun of the stronger keepalive-longerstrong scout checks whether that branch is at least reproducible before it is retired as a non-basin-widening negative.
notes: Exact rerun was a stronger negative than the original longerstrong scout. Test overall=0.4521, route=0.2607, fq_acc=0.2607, fq_route=0.0000, fq_exit=10.73, premature_exit=1.0000. That is well below the original seed1006_p1 result, so the longerstrong branch is not reproducible enough to treat as a real basin-widening improvement.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun of the strongest REINFORCE variant checks whether the cluster-D negative map is stable enough to retire policy-gradient discovery fairly.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the original REINFORCE entropyhigh result numerically on test: overall=0.4391, route=0.2559, fq_acc=0.2320, fq_route=0.0000, fq_exit=1.00. That makes the cluster-D entropyhigh negative reproducible rather than a one-shot miss.

hypothesis: Stronger force-oracle release with a longer schedule may seed more usable late-route structure than the earlier forceoracle branch.
notes: Completed scout improved the old forceoracle boundary enough to keep the branch alive: overall=0.6283, route=0.5778, fq_acc=0.2801, fq_route=0.1912, fq_exit=63.63, best_val=0.6313. Still clearly behind the stronger recovery family, but it seeds real late-route structure rather than collapsing completely. Note: results dir keeps the old seed990 naming convention, but the actual config seed is 1302.

hypothesis: Stronger release/control supervision may give the controlwaitact family a fairer tuned shot before retirement.
notes: Completed scout remained an early-exit failure despite stronger release/control supervision: overall=0.4739, route=0.2288, fq_acc=0.2525, fq_route=0.0000, fq_exit=8.27, best_val=0.4742. This keeps the controlwaitact family on the negative side until the late-unlock variant reports.

hypothesis: Unlocking the controller later may give controlwaitact a cleaner exploration phase before it learns the degenerate early-exit policy.
notes: Completed scout was worse than the releaseaux variant: overall=0.4529, route=0.0876, fq_acc=0.2645, fq_route=0.0000, fq_exit=4.81, best_val=0.4449. The late-unlock schedule did not keep controlwaitact out of the early-exit basin.

hypothesis: Replaying the base force-oracle release under phase-7 accounting gives a direct comparison against the stronger longerstrong branch and checks whether the improvement was real.
notes: Completed replay scout confirms that the stronger forceoracle branch improvement was real. Base replay reached overall=0.6162, route=0.4925, fq_acc=0.2854, fq_route=0.0488, fq_exit=64.79, best_val=0.6089, clearly below longerstrong on route match but still better than pure early-exit collapse.

hypothesis: A 2-GPU hybrid ES resume from the reproduced keepalive discovery anchor may reveal whether ES can rescue a direct-discovery checkpoint that still exits too early.
notes: Completed 2-GPU ES resume converted the reproduced keepalive discovery anchor into a near-perfect late-delay solution. Test overall=0.9756, route=1.0000, fq_acc=0.9500, fq_route=1.0000, fq_exit=127.00. Independent verification matched base metrics exactly and both locked confirmations stayed near-perfect: full-locked 0.9935/0.9900/0.9873/126.53 and finalquery-heavy 0.9906/0.9889/0.9869/126.36. Exact same-seed rerun matched numerically, so this branch has cleared the first two verification rungs.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun of the promising keepalive ES resume is required before this branch can be treated as a real phase-7 result.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the original ES resume numerically on test: overall=0.9756, route=1.0000, fq_acc=0.9500, fq_route=1.0000, fq_exit=127.00. This confirms the keepalive-anchor ES rescue is reproducible at the exact-config level.

hypothesis: If the reproduced medium forceoracle source only needs memory updates, memory-only reopening should preserve late-route structure with less overfitting than wider reopen masks.
notes: Completed direct-source memory-only recovery stayed stable across locked confirmations but did not beat memoryreadout: verify base overall=0.4222, route=0.4707, fq_acc=0.2828, fq_route=0.5321, fq_exit=92.61; full locked=0.4176/0.4720/0.2854/0.5408/92.98; finalquery-heavy=0.3376/0.5078/0.2902/0.5402/93.10.

hypothesis: If sink projection is unnecessary on the reproduced medium source, reopening memory and readout without sink_proj should preserve later exits while slightly improving route fidelity.
notes: Completed direct-source memoryreadout-nosink branch gave the best route-oriented tradeoff of the lighter reopen masks and stayed stable on locked confirms: base overall=0.4131, route=0.4870, fq_acc=0.2848, fq_route=0.5735, fq_exit=99.07; full locked=0.4157/0.4941/0.2868/0.5989/98.65; finalquery-heavy=0.3356/0.5436/0.2874/0.5821/97.86.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun checks whether the medium-source memoryreadout-nosink tradeoff branch is reproducible before promotion.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the first pass on val/test and independent verification. The direct medium-source memoryreadout-nosink branch is reproducible and remains a real tradeoff candidate relative to memoryreadout: verify base route=0.4714, fq_acc=0.2901, fq_route=0.5802; locked confirmations were already stable on the original run.

hypothesis: A middle reopen mask over memory, sink_proj, and readout may recover a bit more task accuracy than memoryreadout-nosink while preserving late-route structure from the reproduced medium source.
notes: Completed direct-source memory+sink+readout branch stayed stable on both locked confirmations but did not beat the stable memoryreadout branch: base overall=0.4373, route=0.4810, fq_acc=0.2692, fq_route=0.5550, fq_exit=94.21; full locked=0.4373/0.4700/0.3011/0.5531/93.53; finalquery-heavy=0.3496/0.5239/0.2894/0.5557/93.37. It earned an exact same-seed rerun because it is stable and sits between memoryonly and memoryreadout-nosink.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun checks whether the medium-source memory+sink+readout branch is reproducible before any promotion decision.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the first pass bit-for-bit on best-val and test: overall=0.4294, route=0.4759, fq_acc=0.2674, fq_route=0.5675, fq_exit=95.58. This makes memory+sink+readout a reproducible but not superior medium-source branch.

hypothesis: If the direct medium-source memoryreadout path is a real basin and not a single refinement-seed accident, adjacent refinement seeds should stay in the same late-route regime.
notes: First additional panel seed stayed in the same medium-source late-route regime instead of collapsing: test overall=0.4489, route=0.4811, fq_acc=0.2828, fq_route=0.5568, fq_exit=95.32. This is slightly weaker than seed1302 but close enough to justify building the rest of the panel.

hypothesis: Additional panel seeds should reveal whether the direct medium-source memoryreadout branch is a stable basin or a narrow one-seed pocket.
notes: Additional panel seed stayed in the same medium-source late-route regime: test overall=0.4453, route=0.4792, fq_acc=0.2861, fq_route=0.5615, fq_exit=95.09.

hypothesis: Additional panel seeds should reveal whether the direct medium-source memoryreadout branch is a stable basin or a narrow one-seed pocket.
notes: Additional panel seed stayed in-family and survived locked confirmations: base overall=0.4246, route=0.4924, fq_acc=0.3090, fq_route=0.5908, fq_exit=98.65; full locked=0.4314/0.4954/0.3206/0.5874/99.04; finalquery-heavy=0.3474/0.5330/0.3025/0.5679/96.77.

hypothesis: A fifth panel seed completes the direct medium-source memoryreadout seed panel and tests whether the late-route regime persists across a wider refinement-seed spread.
notes: Fifth panel seed stayed in the same late-route regime instead of collapsing: test overall=0.4095, route=0.4730, fq_acc=0.2928, fq_route=0.5735, fq_exit=97.33. The five-seed direct branch now averages overall=0.4361±0.0172 and fq_route=0.5691±0.0089 on base test.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun checks whether the strongest new direct medium-source panel member is reproducible before using it as an ES source.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the original seed1305 result bit-for-bit on test: overall=0.4225, route=0.4840, fq_acc=0.3048, fq_route=0.5816, fq_exit=97.95. This makes seed1305 a fair new source for ES follow-up.

hypothesis: If the earlier router-only ES confirmation collapse was specific to the original medium source rather than the whole direct branch, resuming from the stronger confirmed seed1305 source should hold up better.
notes: Promoted 2-GPU router-only ES from the stronger seed1305 source improved the base split only modestly and still collapsed badly on locked confirmations. Verify base overall=0.6304, route=0.8069, fq_acc=0.2746, fq_route=0.6310, fq_exit=92.86; full locked=0.5242/0.4285/0.2587/0.1529/54.13; finalquery-heavy=0.3655/0.2588/0.2565/0.1476/53.31. This makes the confirmation fragility branch-wide for router-only ES, not just a bad-source artifact.

hypothesis: If adapters are the missing stabilizer on the now-confirmed seed1305 source family, source-matched adapter ES should retain more of the base gain on locked confirmations than router-only ES.
notes: Promoted source-matched adapter ES from the confirmed seed1305 source traded raw overall accuracy for much stronger and more stable final-query routing than router-only ES. Verify base overall=0.4395, route=0.7161, fq_acc=0.3191, fq_route=0.9995, fq_exit=126.97; full locked=0.4321/0.5549/0.2844/0.6465/105.22; finalquery-heavy=0.3640/0.6096/0.3104/0.6427/104.56. Compared to router-only from the same source, adapters preserve much more delayed-route structure on confirmations even though overall accuracy remains modest.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun checks whether the adapter ES confirmation-stability gain from the seed1305 source is real or another one-off trajectory.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the adapter ES result bit-for-bit on base test: overall=0.4602, route=0.7153, fq_acc=0.3380, fq_route=0.9980, fq_exit=126.76. Together with the original run's locked confirmations, this makes the source-matched adapter-vs-router-only split a real phase-7 ES finding.

hypothesis: If the adapter-ES confirmation-stability effect is really a property of the stable direct branch rather than only the best seed1305 source, the same recipe should reproduce from another panel member such as seed1306.
notes: Second source-matched adapter ES run from panel seed1306 reproduced the same qualitative pattern as seed1305. Verify base overall=0.4495, route=0.7202, fq_acc=0.3308, fq_route=0.9995, fq_exit=126.97; full locked=0.4375/0.5498/0.2944/0.6341/103.92; finalquery-heavy=0.3577/0.6113/0.3046/0.6467/104.90. This shows the better confirmation story is a property of the stable direct source family, not just one panel member.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun checks whether the adapter-ES confirmation-stability effect also reproduces from a second direct-source panel member.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the seed1306 adapter ES result bit-for-bit on base test: overall=0.4663, route=0.7192, fq_acc=0.3425, fq_route=0.9980, fq_exit=126.76. Together with the original run's locked confirmations, this extends the adapter ES confirmation-stability result to a second direct-source panel member.

hypothesis: If the new adapter-ES confirmation-stability pattern is not tied to one ES random stream, changing the ES seed while keeping the strong source fixed should produce the same qualitative result.
notes: Source-held, ES-seed-changed adapter ES from the confirmed seed1305 source exactly matched the seed1604 branch on base test and locked confirmations. Verify base confirm=0.4395/0.7161/0.3191/0.9995/126.97, full locked test=0.4300/0.5378/0.3001/0.6384/105.66, finalquery-heavy test=0.3457/0.6038/0.2902/0.6371/105.02. This shows the adapter-ES confirmation-stability effect is robust to ES seed for this direct source.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun checks whether the new ES-seed-1605 adapter branch is fully reproducible before promoting it as a real source-and-ES-seed stability result.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the original seed1605 adapter ES result bit-for-bit on base test and locked confirmations: base confirm=0.4395/0.7161/0.3191/0.9995/126.97, full locked test=0.4300/0.5378/0.3001/0.6384/105.66, finalquery-heavy test=0.3457/0.6038/0.2902/0.6371/105.02. Together with the seed1604 and seed1306 runs, this makes the adapter-ES stability claim much tighter.

hypothesis: If the adapter-ES confirmation-stability effect is a branch property rather than a one-source artifact, holding ES seed fixed at 1605 should reproduce from the second stable direct source seed1306 as well.
notes: Cross-factor adapter ES from the second stable direct source seed1306 also matched the earlier seed1604 branch exactly on base test and locked confirmations. Verify base confirm=0.4495/0.7202/0.3308/0.9995/126.97, full locked test=0.4320/0.5342/0.2995/0.6317/105.58, finalquery-heavy test=0.3428/0.6029/0.2898/0.6457/105.87. This makes the adapter-ES confirmation-stability effect robust across both source seed and ES seed within the stable direct branch.

hypothesis: Exact same-seed rerun checks whether the cross-factor adapter-ES result from source seed1306 and ES seed1605 is fully reproducible before treating it as a real grid-level stability result.
notes: Exact same-seed rerun matched the source1306/ES-seed1605 adapter ES result bit-for-bit on base test and locked confirmations: base confirm=0.4495/0.7202/0.3308/0.9995/126.97, full locked test=0.4320/0.5342/0.2995/0.6317/105.58, finalquery-heavy test=0.3428/0.6029/0.2898/0.6457/105.87. Together with the seed1305 grid results, the adapter-ES branch now looks deterministic over this 2x2 source/ES-seed slice.
